title: Define Stored Procedure Components
text: Define Stored Procedure Components After creating a store procedure, you need to define its signature in Uniface. Develop and thoroughly test the DBMS stored procedure. For information on DBMS-specific implementation and operation requirements, see the relevant DBMS driver information. In the Signature Editor, created a new modeled component. Select Stored Procedure Component as the implementation type. Define the operations (and their parameters) associated with the stored procedure. Optionally, enter comments for internal documentation purposes in the appropriate Comment fields. Ensure that the stored procedure component is defined as stateless. This is necessary because a stored procedure is called as a global operation, and not as a single instance. Select File >Compile Signature . Related Links Naming Specifications for Service Store Procedures For Service Stored Procedures, the signature of the Stored Procedure Component maps the names of Uniface components, operations, and parameters to the name of a package, procedure, and parameter names in the procedure. SQL and Stored Procedures Support For SQL-enabled databases, you can use the sql ProcScript instruction and SQL Editor to send SQL instructions to the database. Some databases also support stored procedures. Define Signatures for Service Stored Procedures Create a signature for a service stored procedure, in which one or more stored procedures are represented to Uniface as operations.
